notes: Shankara reads avyayām as 'unending, perpetual'; Ramanuja reads bhūtāni broadly ('all people, qualified and unqualified, for all time'); both restrict the dishonour-worse-than-death claim to sambhāvitasya. The engineering layer preserves all three readings without conflating them. The avyayām-Self-mirror — that the same word used for the Self's imperishability is now used for the actor's manufactured imperishable signal — is doctrinally sharp and the engineering layer surfaces it explicitly. The kṣatriya frame is preserved in the source pack and honestly narrowed in the engineering layer to peer-witness contexts where reputation is built on observable conduct under load. The varṇa-specificity is not collapsed. The engineering vocabulary (peer-network, hiring loop, recruiter intake notes) does not erase the Sanskrit (avyayām, akīrti, sambhāvitasya all appear in the analog). PASS on 7.4 and 6.8.
